articles.title: PM Modi Unveils Six BCCI Indoor Academies Across North-East India
articles.description:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi has today officially inaugurated six state-of-the-art BCCI Indoor Cricket Academies across the North-Eastern states, marking a watershed moment for the sport in the region. The virtual ceremony, held during a state function at the Paljor Stadium in Sikkim, signals a massive push by the Indian cricket board to provide world-class training facilities to players who have historically faced significant infrastructure challenges.

articles.title: Will Lungi Ngidi Return For DC vs RR IPL 2026 Clash? Here's What We Know
articles.description:
Lungi Ngidi Return Update: Delhi Capitals (DC) pacer Lungi Ngidi was involved in a worrying moment during the IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings (PBKS). The incident occurred when Ngidi attempted to take a catch off a lofted stroke from Priyansh Arya. While backtracking to judge the ball, Ngidi failed to get his hands to it and ended up losing balance. In the process, he fell backwards awkwardly and struck his head hard on the ground. The impact immediately raised concern among players and support staff, prompting a lengthy stoppage in play that lasted over 10 minutes.

Medical personnel rushed to attend to the fast bowler before he was eventually stretchered off the field and taken to hospital for further evaluation. In his absence, Vipraj Nigam was introduced as a concussion substitute.

Recovery Positive, But Absence Continues

Although Ngidi was later discharged from hospital and initial reports suggested no serious complications, the pacer has not yet returned to action.

He missed Delhi’s subsequent fixture against Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) and remains doubtful for the upcoming encounter against Rajasthan Royals (RR).

According to a report by Cricbuzz, players diagnosed with concussion are required to observe a mandatory seven-day rest period under ICC guidelines. While the IPL’s playing conditions do not explicitly enforce this rule, it is understood that Ngidi has been advised to follow the same precautionary measure.

This effectively puts his participation in the next match in serious doubt, as teams prioritise player safety in such cases. His absence is concerning for the DC camp, especially given his role in strengthening the bowling attack.

Starc Set To Return?

There is, however, some positive news for Delhi. Australian quick Mitchell Starc, who missed the early part of the season, could be close to making his return against RR. He has already joined the squad, and if available to play, his inclusion could provide a timely boost to the Capitals’ pace attack.

As DC navigate a crucial phase in IPL 2026, Ngidi’s recovery and Starc’s potential comeback could play a decisive role in shaping their campaign.

articles.title: Youth Connect: PM Modi Plays Football with Children in Sikkim
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a visit to Sikkim, where his second day began with a lively and engaging interaction with students in Gangtok. Early in the morning, he visited a football ground and was seen playing football with children, including both boys and girls, reflecting the strong sports culture of the region. Dressed in a sports outfit, the Prime Minister participated in warm-up exercises, played the game, and later interacted warmly with the children, exchanging high-fives and taking photographs. The visuals show him actively engaging with young players, highlighting his focus on youth connection and sports promotion. The event also reflects Sikkim’s growing enthusiasm for football and its vibrant sporting culture. The children appeared excited and happy to share the field with the Prime Minister. Overall, the visit combined sports, interaction, and symbolic outreach as part of Sikkim’s 50-year celebration events.
